definition_origin: GDPR (Regulation (EU) 2016/679, in force 25 May 2018) Article 5(1)(c) codified the data minimization principle: 'personal data shall be adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary in relation to the purposes for which they are processed.' CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act, in force 1 January 2020) and CPRA (California Privacy Rights Act, in force 1 January 2023) imported similar principles for California residents. As of 2025, 71% of countries have GDPR-style legislation (UNCTAD Global Privacy Tracker 2025) — the framework has become global default. UK GDPR (post-Brexit, mirrors EU GDPR), Brazil LGPD (2020), India DPDPA (2023, in force 2024), Japan APPI (amended 2022, 2024), Korea PIPA (amended 2023), and Australia Privacy Act (amendments 2024) all carry data-minimization provisions. The onboarding-specific application — that questionnaire screens cannot collect data 'just in case' — is enforced via Apple App Store Guideline 5.1.1(v) (2024-2025 strengthening) and EU DPA enforcement actions (Italian Garante fined Replika €5M Feb 2025 for excessive onboarding data collection).
core_mechanism: Three regulatory mechanisms. (1) PURPOSE LIMITATION (GDPR Art 5(1)(b)) — data must be collected for 'specified, explicit and legitimate purposes' and not further processed beyond. Onboarding cannot ask 'just in case we need it later.' (2) DATA MINIMIZATION (GDPR Art 5(1)(c)) — only data 'necessary' for the stated purpose. The 'necessary' standard is interpreted strictly by EU DPAs — convenience or business-intelligence is not 'necessary.' (3) LAWFUL BASIS (GDPR Art 6) — collection requires one of six bases (consent, contract, legal obligation, vital interests, public task, legitimate interest). For onboarding personalization, consent is the typical basis; consent must be 'freely given, specific, informed and unambiguous' (GDPR Art 4(11)). The onboarding-design implication: every questionnaire field must justify itself against (a) what specific app function uses it, (b) consent disclosed before collection, (c) minimum-necessary scope. CCPA/CPRA enforce similar but with opt-out (US) vs opt-in (EU) defaults.
empirical_evidence: GDPR enforcement 2018-2025: cumulative fines exceed €5.4 billion across all regulators (CMS GDPR Enforcement Tracker, Q1 2025). Notable onboarding-related fines: Replika fined €5M by Italian Garante Feb 2025 for excessive onboarding personal data collection without proper consent + age verification. Meta fined €1.2B by Irish DPC 2023 for cross-border data transfers (onboarding-related data flows). Google fined €50M by French CNIL 2019 for onboarding consent UI patterns. Amazon fined €746M by Luxembourg CNPD 2021. CCPA enforcement: California AG actions 2024-2025, including against Sephora ($1.2M settlement re: opt-out notice), DoorDash ($375K), HotelTonight, and others. Brazilian ANPD enforcement 2024-2025 (LGPD) against Telegram, Meta. UK ICO 2024-2025 enforcement under UK GDPR. European Data Protection Board 2024-2025 guidelines on consent in onboarding flows specifically: cookie banner standards extended to mobile onboarding consent dialogs. UNCTAD Global Privacy Tracker 2025: 162 countries (71% of 227 countries surveyed) have data protection laws. Apple App Store enforcement under Guideline 5.1.1(v): rejection rate for onboarding data-minimization issues increased ~40% in 2024-2025 per RevenueCat App Store Rejections guide.
measurable_outcome_metric: Onboarding data fields necessity score (qualitative — each field justified against app function). Privacy notice readability score (Flesch-Kincaid <8th grade). Consent-acceptance rate per-region (per GDPR opt-in vs CCPA opt-out). Number of onboarding fields collected (lower = better minimization). DPA enforcement actions or complaints received (defensive metric).
conversion_lift_benchmark: Not a conversion lift item — defensive compliance. Fines: GDPR up to 4% of global annual turnover OR €20M (whichever higher); CCPA $2,500 per violation, $7,500 per intentional violation; LGPD up to 2% of Brazilian revenue; UK GDPR same as EU (post-Brexit). EU DPA Replika €5M fine (Feb 2025) demonstrates onboarding-specific enforcement is real. Indirect conversion impact: shorter onboarding (fewer questions) often paradoxically converts BETTER (lower friction) AND complies BETTER — RevenueCat 2024 data shows app installs correlate negatively with onboarding length beyond ~7-15 questions.
engineering_tactics: ARCHITECTURAL CHANGES: (1) Field-by-field necessity audit — each onboarding field mapped to specific app function: 'name → personalization', 'email → magic-link auth + transactional email', 'birth-date → COPPA age-gate + age-targeted content', 'weight → BMI calculation for macro plan'. Drop fields that don't map. (2) Consent UI BEFORE first data-collection field, not at end of onboarding. Per-purpose consent (separate toggle for analytics vs personalization vs marketing) — bundled consent is non-compliant under GDPR Art 7(2). (3) Privacy notice in user's language, ≥14pt, accessible from every onboarding screen via 'i' icon or footer link. (4) Right to withdraw consent must be 'as easy as giving' (Art 7(3)) — Settings > Privacy > Withdraw. (5) Lawful basis tracking server-side: per-field record of basis (consent | contract | legitimate_interest) + timestamp. (6) Data portability + erasure (Art 20, 17) infrastructure: export-data + delete-account endpoints accessible in 2 taps from app launch. (7) Children data (under 16 EU / 13 US) requires parental consent — branch to COPPA flow (item 48). (8) Cross-border data transfer compliance: Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) for EU→US, EU-US Data Privacy Framework (post-Schrems II, in force July 2023). Implementation pattern (RN + i18next): const consentState = await loadConsentState(); if (!consentState.has_consented_for_onboarding) { showConsentModal(); }. Telemetry consent: send analytics events ONLY after analytics_consent === true.
real_world_examples: 1) Apple's onboarding (any app) — consent disclosure on first launch via App Tracking Transparency (item 47) + per-app data labels in App Store. 2) Cal AI — recently audited onboarding to drop 'job title' and 'income' fields after 2024 EU consent inquiries; now collects only weight/height/goal — directly necessary for macro plan. 3) Headspace — clear per-purpose consent (analytics / personalization / marketing) at onboarding start; UK ICO compliant. 4) Duolingo — onboarding asks ONLY language pair, daily goal, level — minimum necessary for language matching. 5) Headway — collects reading interests + reading speed only; dropped 'occupation' field 2023-2024 post-GDPR audit. 6) Strava — Italian Garante 2024 inquiry into excessive location-tracking onboarding consent led to redesign; now opt-in per-feature. 7) Replika — Italian Garante €5M fine Feb 2025 for excessive emotional-data collection without proper minor age-gate; required redesign. 8) Counter-example: TikTok Italian Garante €10M fine 2021 for inadequate age-gate and consent UI in onboarding — DO NOT REPLICATE the pre-2021 TikTok onboarding pattern. 9) Notion — minimal onboarding (workspace name only); collects more later only when needed for features.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary in 2025-2026. Apple App Store Privacy Labels (since iOS 14) require self-declaration of all data collection in onboarding + ongoing — auditable. Apple App Tracking Transparency (item 47) layers on GDPR consent. Google Play Data Safety section (since 2022) — equivalent to Apple Privacy Labels. Web: cookie banner regime (ePrivacy Directive) overlaps with GDPR; consent-management platforms (Axeptio, OneTrust, Usercentrics, Cookiebot) inject geo-aware consent UI. Mobile = apps must implement equivalent in-app consent UI (no third-party CMP injection — must build natively).
platform_design_language_alignment: Liquid Glass alert sheets (iOS 26+) work well for consent disclosure modals — bolder left-aligned typography for purpose disclosures, translucent material. M3 Expressive: full-screen consent screen with M3 large typography for purpose emphasis + per-purpose toggle. Apple HIG explicitly addresses privacy-sensitive UI patterns: mandatory disclosure modals, pre-permission education screens. Material 3 has equivalent.
category_fit: Universal — every app collecting personal data must comply. Especially critical for: wellness/health (sensitive data category), kids/family (COPPA + GDPR-K + UK Children's Code overlay), fintech (additional sectoral regulation), AI-wrapper (training-data implications), social/dating (location + communications). Lower-stakes: pure offline utilities, but even those need privacy notice if collecting analytics.
solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week for compliant onboarding redesign. ~2 days field-necessity audit + drop unjustified fields. ~2 days per-purpose consent UI + i18next privacy notice translation. ~1 day data-export + delete-account endpoints (Supabase RLS + Postgres queries). ~1 day server-side consent timestamp logging. Solo-dev shortcut: ship absolute minimum onboarding (3-5 fields, all clearly necessary) + Apple App Privacy Labels accurate + Settings > Privacy > Export/Delete + per-purpose consent at onboarding start.
tooling_options: Axeptio (consent management; free <100K monthly visits). OneTrust (enterprise). Usercentrics. Cookiebot. Termly / iubenda (privacy notice templates with per-region compliance). Supabase / PostgreSQL Row-Level Security for data-export + delete (free tier). Apple App Store Connect Privacy Labels (native, free). Google Play Data Safety section (native, free). Datadog / Sentry data-residency configuration (EU-region for EU users). i18next for multilingual privacy notices. SendGrid / Postmark for data-export delivery (transactional email).
ai_dependency: none for compliance mechanism; optional for privacy notice generation (LLMs draft notices but require legal review).
claude_design_application: Prompt fragment 1: 'Audit this React Native onboarding flow for GDPR Article 5(1)(c) data minimization compliance. For each questionnaire field, output: (a) field name, (b) specific app function that requires it, (c) whether it can be dropped (yes/no/with-feature-loss), (d) lawful basis (consent | contract | legitimate_interest), (e) sensitive-data flag (GDPR Art 9). Drop fields that fail necessity test. Output: refactored field list + consent-UI placement (BEFORE first data-collection field, per-purpose toggles, not bundled). Add Settings > Privacy > Export Data + Delete Account flows accessible in 2 taps from app launch.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Generate per-purpose consent UI screen: per-purpose toggles for Analytics + Personalization + Marketing (separate, not bundled). Toggles default OFF for EU users (opt-in); CCPA users see opt-out version. Privacy notice link in ≥14pt body text accessible from every onboarding screen. Translation: i18next strings with native-speaker review for EU + UK languages. Logged consent server-side with timestamp + lawful_basis + purpose for each toggle.'
stitch_application: Prompt fragment 1: 'Generate the per-purpose consent screen for an onboarding flow. Header Söhne 24pt bold left: How we use your data. Subtitle Manrope 16pt: You are in control. Below: 3 separate toggles (NOT bundled) — Analytics: Help us improve the app (default off EU / opt-out CCPA), Personalization: Tailor your experience, Marketing emails: Updates and offers. Each toggle has 1-line plain-English purpose explanation Söhne 14pt. Privacy notice link Manrope 14pt grey-700 underlined: Read full Privacy Notice. Primary CTA Continue 56pt height. Forbid: bundled consent (single Accept All button = GDPR violation), pre-checked boxes (Article 7), 8pt grey footnote text, indigo-500 toggles. Use platform-native iOS toggles + Cabinet Grotesk + Manrope.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Generate the Settings > Privacy screen with Export Data and Delete Account buttons. Each button is filled brand-accent 56pt height with confirmation modal. Export delivers email within 24h (transactional Postmark). Delete is irreversible with 7-day grace period. Both compliant with GDPR Art 15 (access) + Art 17 (erasure) + Art 20 (portability). CCPA equivalent rights covered. Forbid: hiding behind 4 sub-menu levels, retention modals before delete, double-negative button copy.'
ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI defaults: (1) Bundled 'Accept All' consent button (GDPR Art 7(2) violation). (2) Pre-checked consent boxes (GDPR Art 4(11) violation — not unambiguous). (3) Privacy notice in 8pt grey footer with toFixed-style hardcoded English (locale + accessibility violation). (4) Asks fields 'just in case' — birth-date when not needed, occupation when not needed, full name when first-name suffices. (5) No data-export / delete-account endpoint (GDPR Art 15 + 17 violation). (6) Bundled analytics + personalization consent (must be per-purpose). (7) Consent UI AFTER data already collected (must be BEFORE). (8) No lawful-basis logging server-side. Counter-prompt: 'GDPR Article 5(1)(c) data minimization — every field must have justified app-function necessity. GDPR Article 7 consent — per-purpose toggles (NOT bundled), default-OFF for EU, before first data collection. Privacy notice in user language ≥14pt accessible from every screen. Settings > Privacy > Export Data + Delete Account — 2 taps from app launch. Server-side consent log: timestamp + purpose + lawful_basis. Forbid Accept All bundled consent, pre-checked boxes, asking fields not justified by feature.'
anti_patterns: 1) Bundled 'Accept All' consent button (Art 7(2) violation). 2) Pre-checked consent boxes (Art 4(11) violation). 3) Asking fields 'just in case' (Art 5(1)(c) violation). 4) Consent dialog AFTER first data collection (Art 6 violation). 5) Privacy notice in 8pt grey footer (Art 12(1) clear-and-plain-language violation). 6) Hidden delete-account flow (Art 17 violation). 7) Cross-border data transfer without SCCs / DPF (Schrems II + Art 44 violation). 8) Bundled analytics + personalization + marketing consent (per-purpose required). 9) Children data without parental consent (Art 8 + COPPA violation). 10) Sensitive data (health, biometric) without explicit consent (Art 9 violation). 11) Consent withdrawal harder than giving (Art 7(3) violation).
regulatory_risk: HIGH. GDPR fines: up to 4% global annual turnover OR €20M whichever higher (Replika €5M Feb 2025 = onboarding-specific precedent). CCPA: $2,500 per violation, $7,500 intentional. CPRA: enforcement by California Privacy Protection Agency (active since 2023). LGPD (Brazil): up to 2% revenue. UK GDPR: same as EU. India DPDPA: ₹250 crore (~$30M). EU DFA Q4 2026 — additional layer on consent UIs and dark patterns. UK Children's Code Standard 7 (data minimization for under-18s). FTC Section 5 — unfair and deceptive practices for non-EU users.
platform_constraints_2026: Apple App Store Guideline 5.1.1(v) — privacy and data minimization; rejection rate +40% in 2024-2025 for onboarding violations. Apple App Privacy Labels (since iOS 14) — must accurately declare data collection. Google Play Data Safety section (since 2022) — equivalent. Apple ATT Framework (item 47) — separate consent layer for IDFA tracking. Apple Guideline 1.1.6 — data sharing with third parties limited.
accessibility_implications: Privacy notice + consent UI must meet WCAG 2.2: 4.5:1 contrast, ≥14pt body text (NOT 8pt grey), VoiceOver/TalkBack accessible (per-purpose toggles labeled clearly), Dynamic Type 200%, keyboard accessible. SC 3.3.2 Labels or Instructions — toggle purposes plain-language. SC 1.4.4 Resize Text — privacy notice scales without loss of meaning. Toggle state must be announced (VoiceOver: 'Analytics, switch, off, double-tap to turn on').
pitfalls_caveats: 1) GDPR vs CCPA defaults inverted (EU opt-in vs CA opt-out) — geo-detection required for correct default. 2) Schrems II — EU→US data transfers require SCCs OR DPF certification. 3) Sensitive data (Art 9) — health, biometric, religious requires explicit consent + heightened standard. 4) Children data Art 8 EU — varies 13-16 by member state. 5) Right-to-be-forgotten edge cases (legal hold, financial records). 6) Consent withdrawal must be as easy as giving — Settings > Privacy > Manage Consent. 7) Per-region privacy notice translation requires native review. 8) Server-side consent log retention (typically 4-7 years for audit trail). 9) Apple ATT consent (item 47) is SEPARATE from GDPR consent — both required.
cross_cultural_validity: 71% of countries have GDPR-style legislation (UNCTAD 2025). EU GDPR strictest baseline; UK GDPR same; Brazil LGPD nearly identical; Japan APPI similar with stricter sectoral rules; Korea PIPA similar; India DPDPA (2023) GDPR-influenced. Strict regimes: EU, UK, Brazil, Japan, Korea, India, Australia, Canada PIPEDA. Less strict: US (sectoral; CCPA + state laws), but California strict. Apply EU GDPR as strictest baseline globally — meeting GDPR usually exceeds others. RTL: privacy notice layout mirror.
prescribed_action: Audit your onboarding NOW: (1) Drop every field that doesn't map to a specific named app function (data minimization). (2) Move per-purpose consent UI (Analytics / Personalization / Marketing as separate toggles, NOT bundled) to BEFORE first data-collection field. EU users: default off. (3) Privacy notice in user's language ≥14pt body, accessible from every onboarding screen. (4) Settings > Privacy > Export Data + Delete Account in 2 taps from app launch (Art 15 + 17 + 20). (5) Server-side log: per-field lawful_basis + consent timestamp + purpose for audit trail. (6) Apple App Privacy Labels + Google Play Data Safety self-declarations accurate. (7) Cross-border transfer: SCCs OR EU-US DPF certification. (8) Children flow branches to COPPA (item 48) for under-16 EU / under-13 US.
master_prompt_relevance: 06_monetization_compliance (primary), 02_design (consent UI typography + accessibility), 04_implementation (consent log + export/delete endpoints), 03_architecture (lawful_basis state machine).
questionnaire_relevance: Questionnaire is the PRIMARY data-collection surface in onboarding. Every question must justify itself against (a) what specific app function uses the answer, (b) consent disclosed before collection, (c) minimum-necessary scope. Drop questions that fail. Sequence consent UI BEFORE first data-collection question.
aha_relevance: N/A directly — but compliant minimum-necessary onboarding (5-7 questions vs 33) reduces friction AND complies AND can paradoxically increase Aha quality (faster path to AI plan reveal).
telemetry_events: consent_modal_viewed (locale, region), consent_purpose_toggled (purpose, value), consent_completed (purposes_accepted, timestamp), data_export_requested, data_export_delivered, account_deletion_requested, account_deletion_completed, consent_withdrawn (purpose, timestamp), data_field_collected (field_name, lawful_basis, purpose).
measurement_horizon: Day 0 (compliance gate at consent modal; cannot collect data before consent). Ongoing (consent state mutable per-user; audit log retained 4-7 years). Annual (privacy notice review; field necessity re-audit).
emotional_archetype_alignment: Caregiver (privacy = care for user), Sage (informed consent = wisdom), Innocent (transparency = trust). Conflicts with Outlaw / Ruler if those interpret privacy regulation as anti-business — but compliance is non-negotiable; archetype voice can express disclosure warmly while complying.
anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against: Bundled Accept-All Consent, Pre-Checked Boxes, Hidden Material Terms, Asking Just-in-Case Fields, Hidden Delete-Account Flow, Cross-Border Transfer Without SCCs. Aligns with: COPPA Age Gate (item 48), CMP / ATT Framework (item 47), Click-to-Cancel Compliance (item 50), DFA Audit (item 49).
